edges.description: Loss of APRT activity leaves adenine available for oxidation by xanthine oxidoreductase.
Biallelic APRT loss of function is measured diagnostically as absent or markedly reduced enzyme activity.
The proximal enzymatic defect is the biochemical phenotype recorded for APRT deficiency.
Poorly soluble DHA is excreted in urine and precipitates as crystals.
XOR-mediated DHA generation produces excessive urinary DHA excretion.
Urinary DHA precipitation produces urolithiasis and obstructive stone events.
DHA crystals deposit within renal tubules and can obstruct tubular lumens.
edges.target: Renal tubular crystal deposition and obstruction

edges.description: DHA stone formation manifests clinically as nephrolithiasis.
Radiolucent DHA stones may be misidentified as uric acid calculi.
Bilateral obstructive DHA stone disease can cause AKI episodes.
Stone disease and urinary tract obstruction can predispose to recurrent urinary tract infections.
DHA stone disease and urinary tract irritation can present with hematuria.
Urinary tract irritation from DHA stones and crystalluria can produce painful urination.
Obstructive stone events can present with flank pain.
Colicky abdominal pain can accompany urinary tract stone events.
Obstructive urinary stone disease can contribute to urinary retention.
Lower urinary tract obstruction and irritation can manifest as urinary hesitancy.
Crystal deposition drives tubular injury and chronic inflammatory-fibrotic repair.
edges.source: Renal tubular crystal deposition and obstruction

edges.description: Chronic crystal nephropathy causes CKD, AKI episodes, and kidney failure risk.
edges.target: Progressive DHA crystal nephropathy and renal dysfunction

edges.description: Biopsy-proven crystal nephropathy can present with elevated urine protein:creatinine ratio.
Progressive DHA crystal nephropathy reduces renal function and manifests as CKD.
edges.source: Progressive DHA crystal nephropathy and renal dysfunction

edges.description: Crystal nephropathy-associated renal dysfunction appears clinically as renal insufficiency.
edges.source: Progressive DHA crystal nephropathy and renal dysfunction

edges.description: Untreated or inadequately treated DHA crystal nephropathy can progress to ESRD.
edges.source: Progressive DHA crystal nephropathy and renal dysfunction

edges.description: Advanced renal dysfunction or obstructive AKI can reduce urine output.
edges.source: Progressive DHA crystal nephropathy and renal dysfunction

edges.description: Chronic kidney disease and renal dysfunction can contribute to hypertension.

edges.description: Absent red-cell APRT activity directly reports the proximal APRT molecular function deficiency.

evidence.snippet: In the absence of APRT activity, adenine is converted by xanthine oxidoreductase (XOR; xanthine dehydrogenase/oxidase) to the poorly soluble 2,8-dihydroxyadenine (DHA) which is excreted in the urine in excessive amounts.

readouts.interpretation: Increased urinary 2,8-DHA reports the abnormal XOR-mediated adenine conversion caused by APRT loss.

nodes.description: Dialysis and kidney transplantation are used for ESRD, but XOR inhibitor therapy remains recommended after transplantation to prevent recurrent DHA nephropathy in the allograft.

evidence.snippet: ESRD is treated with dialysis and kidney transplantation.
Even after kidney transplantation, treatment with an XOR is recommended.

nodes.description: Allopurinol or febuxostat inhibits xanthine oxidoreductase, reducing conversion of adenine to 2,8-DHA, lowering urinary DHA burden, and reducing stone recurrence and kidney injury risk when taken at adequate dose.
